frameworks.name: shared spatial topology of peripheral vision
frameworks.one_line: single underlying visual-field map governs multiple peripheral tasks

frameworks.name: crowding
frameworks.one_line: deleterious effect of clutter on peripheral object recognition

frameworks.name: principal-component analysis
frameworks.one_line: used to isolate shared inter-position variance across tasks

core_question: Do crowding, saccadic precision, and spatial localization share a common topology across the visual field?
key_assumptions: task thresholds at matched eccentric locations are comparable across tasks
inter-position variance reflects underlying spatial map structure
independent task-specific noise would yield uncorrelated topologies
manipulations: task type (letter crowding, saccadic landing, spatial localization)
key_findings: the three task thresholds co-varied across visual-field positions within each observer
one dominant PCA component captured ~75% of inter-position variance
this dominant component was shared across all three tasks
interpretations: peripheral crowding, saccadic precision, and spatial localization are linked by a common underlying spatial topology
task variations are not driven by independent task-specific noise sources
